title: fix: attribute replicate network failures and classify E003 capacity errors as 503
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Wraps fetch/body-read rejections in `ReplicateError` carrying the endpoint URL, threaded through all Replicate model catch blocks into `HttpError.upstreamUrl` — network-level drops ("Network connection lost") previously produced bare 500s with empty `upstream_host`/`upstream_status` in Tinybird, making the wan-image-pro degradation alert undiagnosable
- `fetchUpstream` rethrows fetcher rejections as `HttpError` 502 with the URL; wan output `arrayBuffer()` read is wrapped so mid-download drops name `replicate.delivery`
- Classifies prediction failures with `E003` / "unavailable due to high demand" (Alibaba capacity errors behind Replicate's WAN models) as 503 instead of 500, so monitoring separates provider capacity incidents from our bugs — verified live against prod: wan-image-pro has been failing with E003 since ~21:00 UTC Jul 15 while wan-image on the same token works fine
- Tests for all new paths; 14/14 pass

gist.headline: The upstream gets a name
gist.blurb: When a Replicate connection drops mid-flight, the hive now leaves a useful note instead of pointing vaguely at the weather. WAN capacity traffic also gets its own proper 503 lane.
gist.summary: Replicate fetch and download failures now retain their upstream URL as 502 errors, while WAN prediction failures marked E003 or "unavailable due to high demand" are classified as 503 provider-capacity errors instead of generic 500s.
gist.impact: Users and operators get clearer upstream failure responses, and monitoring can distinguish Replicate/Alibaba WAN capacity incidents from pollinations.ai application errors.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy garden delivery scene where Polly identifies the source of a stalled WAN image parcel: a labeled Replicate delivery tube has encountered a gentle "CAPACITY" sign, while a retro monitor records the correct 503 status. This represents preserving upstream attribution and separating provider demand from internal failures.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene in a warm garden hackerspace: Polly, the cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body, dark brown stripes, black antennae and oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks, a small happy smile, rounded light-blue translucent wings, and a tiny courier cap, carefully labels a stalled lime green (#ecf874) delivery tube "REPLICATE" beside a small pixel sign reading "503 CAPACITY"; a friendly CRT monitor robot logs the route while code-plants and flowers grow around them. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley or A Short Hike, dark purple (#110518) outlines, emotionally warm and gently amused.
